__fixture__.name: startup_restore_does_not_starve_foreground
__fixture__.scenario: At session startup, the queue holds one startup.workspace_restore job plus two provider.overlay_refresh and one knowledge.hot_set_scan job. The user presses a key in an already-open editor. The foreground lane admits the keystroke on the hot_path_interactive_budget and the interactive_background lane continues to run startup restore from its last checkpoint without preempting the keystroke. This diagnostic sample is taken from the interactive_background lane and shows it in the warming projection with lane_depth > 0, while the editor shell's primary visible state remains ready. The sample proves steal_shell_ready_budget = false: the startup lane cannot borrow hot-path capacity.

last_checkpoint.note: Startup restore is mid-phase; layout is restored, index resume is pending.

note: Startup restore and hot-set scan are warming on the knowledge_refresh_budget. The user's first keystroke admits to the foreground lane on hot_path_interactive_budget without waiting for any startup or provider-overlay job; editor_shell projects ready.
